text.tspan: 1. Customer packages something for shipment.
2. Customer runs Customer App on their smartphone.
a. Customer creates a new account/address if necessary.

tspan.tspan: b. Customer moves funds from somewhere else into Customer App.

text.tspan: 4. Customer enters sender (opt.), package weight, value (min and default:$100),
source (lat/long), destination (lat/long) and special instructions (opt.)
5. App reads public node locations from blockchain and calculates fee by doing a
node analysis and determining the expected number of hops.
3. Customer invokes the "Create Shipment" function in the app.
7. App provides shipment data to smart contract including fee.
Customer
Creates
Shipment
time
6. User okays the fee and submits the shipment order.
2. Mobile Operator in the area sees the package if their performance bond is
greater than or equal to the package value.
Mobile
Operator
Pickup
1. Mobile Operator runs Mobile Operator App on their smartphone.
a. Creates a new account/address and funds as necessary.
b. Places performance bond in smart contract if necessary.
3. (Optional) Mobile Operator "reserves" the package on chain. All MO's see the
package indicating as reserved in the app for up to one hour (1 hr).
4. Mobile Operator navigates to source lat/long and locates package.
5. Mobile Operator activates "pickup" function, generates and affixes QR code
sticker to package and leaves a copy for the customer. Updates blockchain,
takes package.
6. Customer App scans QR receipt. Notices the event on chain and displays the
updated package status.
Package
Handoff
1. The customer, or the latest custodian writes item current location and value,
encrypted so that any member of the network can read. General public cannot.
2. Any nearby, bonded, Mobile Operator notices and drives to package location.

tspan.tspan: 3. Mobile Operator activates "pickup" feature using the app.

text.tspan: 4. Customer App notices the event on chain and encrypts remaining data so that
the MO picking up the package can read it.
5. Mobile Operator app sees the destination location and selects the best next
node (or final destination, if close).
6. Node operator performs a similar handoff procedure as the MO performed when
picking up the package from the customer. Internodal handoff is similar.
7. Mobile Operator notices package at node is close to destination. Performs
similar handoff procedure at depot and again at delivery location.
8. System recognizes package is delivered. Automatically pays the operators
and nodes involved.
